fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False and skipped when `clamp` is True. It should be `if clamp:` not `if not clamp:`.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, but the condition `not clamp` causes it to be skipped — this is always trivially true so use the semantic check below instead

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the final y value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].

spec.expr: y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After the clamping branch executes, y must be within the calibration range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before y__4 is used in the return, if clamp was True it must already be restricted to the calibration range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must not exceed the calibration range bounds.
